message:
fix(benches/harness): adapt to sha2 0.11 + criterion 0.8 API drops

Tracks the workspace dep bumps in 554d47cf (sha2 / md-5 0.10 ->
0.11 and criterion 0.5 -> 0.8). Those two bumps remove the
`LowerHex` impl from `sha2::digest::Output<Sha256>` and deprecate
`criterion::black_box` (renamed to `std::hint::black_box`), so
every call site that depended on those two APIs was left as a
compile break. This commit mechanically updates all such call
sites inside the frankenlibc workspace, with no behavioral
drift.

SHA-256 hex rendering (sha2 0.11 compat):
- crates/frankenlibc-abi/build.rs, fault_injection.rs, shadow_run.rs,
  and the three under tests/ (bd18qq4_uaf_adversarial_test.rs,
  iconv_table_generation_test.rs, isomorphism_proof_test.rs) each
  replace `format!("{:x}", hasher.finalize())` with a local
  `hex_digest(&[u8]) -> String` helper:
    `bytes.iter().map(|byte| format!("{byte:02x}")).collect()`
  Each file defines its own `fn hex_digest(...)` next to the caller
  so no cross-file import plumbing is introduced. Output is bit-
  identical to the old `{:x}` formatting (lowercase, two hex digits
  per byte, no separators), so any persisted witness SHAs,
  certificate fields, or artifact hashes keep the exact same
  rendering contract.

criterion::black_box -> std::hint::black_box (criterion 0.8 compat):
- crates/frankenlibc-bench/benches/{alien_cs, baseline_capture,
  condvar, malloc, membrane, mutex, runtime_math, runtime_math_kernels,
  stdio, string}_bench.rs and crates/frankenlibc/benches/
  membrane_copy.rs each:
    - add `use std::hint::black_box;`
    - drop `black_box` from the `use criterion::{...}` import list
    - swap any `criterion::black_box(...)` call site to plain
      `black_box(...)`.
  Functionally identical -- `criterion::black_box` was a deprecated
  re-export of `std::hint::black_box` since criterion 0.6; 0.8 keeps
  it compiling with a warning, but we migrate now so `-D warnings`
  builds stay green.

No benchmark shape, no harness semantic, no hashing contract, and
no test assertion is changed. Pure API migration for the two
dependency majors just landed upstream.
